compiled_state.title: As a DSO plugin user, I can find clear documentation on how to keep my artifacts in sync after plugin updates
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Update `plugins/dso/docs/INSTALL.md` to document the artifact version stamp mechanism, the session-start notification, the `dso update-artifacts` command, and the `claude plugin sync` prerequisite.

**Why**: Without documentation, users who see session-start notifications or encounter CI YAML conflict JSON will not know what to do. The documentation completes the user experience delivered by S1–S3.

**Scope**:
- IN: Updates to `plugins/dso/docs/INSTALL.md` covering: (a) version stamp mechanism, (b) session-start notification behavior, (c) `dso update-artifacts` command with example success and conflict output, (d) `claude plugin sync` prerequisite, (e) legacy/no-stamp migration notice
- IN: Documentation accurate against implemented S1–S3 behavior (no phantom flags or wrong command syntax)
- OUT: Creating new documentation files — updates existing INSTALL.md only
- OUT: CLAUDE.md or other internal docs — those are covered by the project docs update story

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `plugins/dso/docs/INSTALL.md` includes a section explaining the `# dso-version:` / `x-dso-version:` stamp mechanism and how it enables staleness detection
  ← Satisfies: "Users understand how the version stamp mechanism works"
- When this story is complete, the documentation explains the session-start notification behavior: when it fires, when it exits silently, the 24h cache behavior, and the plugin-source-repo silent-exit guard
  ← Satisfies: "Users understand why they received a session-start notification"
- When this story is complete, the documentation includes example output for `dso update-artifacts` in both the success case (artifacts updated) and the conflict case (exit 2 + JSON output), and explains that conflict JSON encodes the conflicting sections as base64 for LLM inspection
  ← Satisfies: "Users know how to use `dso update-artifacts` and what to do when a conflict occurs"
- When this story is complete, the documentation explicitly states that `claude plugin sync` must be run before `dso update-artifacts` to pull the latest plugin version locally
  ← Satisfies: "Users know the correct update workflow sequence"
- When this story is complete, the documentation covers the legacy artifact case: artifacts installed before this epic have no stamp → "legacy/unknown" designation → one-time migration notice at session start → run `dso update-artifacts` to stamp and sync
  ← Satisfies: "Users with legacy installations understand the migration path"
- When this story is complete, all commands and flags documented are verified against the S1–S3 implementation (no phantom flags or incorrect syntax)
  ← Satisfies: "Documentation is accurate and usable"

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Follow `.claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md` for formatting, structure, and conventions

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Fixed: All 6 done definitions satisfied. INSTALL.md covers stamps, notifications, update command, plugin sync, and legacy migration.
comments.author: Test
